about.title: About
about.intro: My Top Words is a small tool for learning vocabulary in any language. The idea is simple: work with words in sentences and practice them.
about.featuresLead: The service is aimed at regular work with vocabulary.
about.repetitionText: In practice, words not yet marked as learned appear again during a session.
about.publicSetsText: Authors can make sets public; other users can view and save them.
about.feature1Text: Every word comes with an example in a sentence — vocabulary is learned in context.
about.feature2Text: Translation exercises help check understanding and reinforce the material.
about.feature3Text: You can create your own sets, save others' public sets, and mark learned words.
about.feature4Text: You can listen to the set language pronunciation during practice.
about.step1Text: Browse public sets or create your own.
about.step2Text: Translate a sentence and check the answer.
about.step3Text: Mark words you already know to focus on the rest.
about.feedbackTextFab: Report UI typos, bugs, or suggestions with the feedback button available on every page.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.subtitle: A brief overview of how the service works and what to know before signing up.
terms.whyText: My Top Words is a tool for learning vocabulary: word sets, practice, and progress tracking. Using the site means this learning format suits you.
terms.dataText1: The account needs only the basics: a username, a protected password (the database stores not the password itself but its cryptographic hash), your word sets, and marks of learned items.
terms.dataText2: If you send feedback, the message text, its type and, optionally, the address of the page where you noticed the problem are stored.
terms.dataText3: We do not sell this data to third parties and do not use it for advertising.
terms.accountText1: The password is your personal responsibility. Choose a strong password and do not share it with others. During registration the site will show complexity requirements — this helps protect the account.
terms.accountText2: All actions performed under your name after logging in are considered yours. If the password becomes known to someone else, it is better to change it or reach out via the feedback button on any page.
terms.materialsText1: Translations and example sentences are for learning purposes. Inaccuracies are possible — the material is convenient for work, study and everyday communication; for official texts you should verify with other sources.
terms.materialsText2: Public sets are created by their authors. By saving someone else's set, you use it within the service's capabilities.
terms.codeText: The client part of the project is distributed under the terms of
terms.codeLicenseNote: The license text is in the LICENSE file of the repository.
terms.changesText: Site features may gradually change. The current version of this text is always here, on this page.
terms.contactText: For questions, suggestions or errors, use the feedback button — it is available on every page.
terms.moreText: More about the service features — on the

setEditor.title: Set contents
setEditor.intro: Each line is one word. Add, edit, or delete lines, then save.
setEditor.columnsHint: Each line: {{columns}}. Column delimiter is | only.
setEditor.confirmDelete: Delete {{removed}} words (their progress will be lost). Add {{added}}. Update {{updated}}?
setEditor.dirtyExitConfirm: You have unsaved changes. Leave without saving?

setRemark.title: Remark about this set
setRemark.hint: Select a text fragment (or describe the issue) — it will be sent to the set owner.
setRemark.inboxIntro: Remarks from users about your sets.
